title: rubus × pseudoeglandulosus
intro: Rubus × pseudoeglandulosus, also known as the pseudoeglandulose black raspberry, is a hybrid plant species within the Rubus genus, derived from a cross between the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help to combat certain diseases. The health benefits of Rubus × pseudoeglandulosus are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, which are known to have anti-cancer and cardiovascular-protective effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically obtained through extraction methods such as infusion or decoction, often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
